data.title: As an agent, I can merge to main via PR instead of direct push
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Rewrite merge-to-main.sh for a PR-based workflow: create PR, poll CI, merge via GitHub API, update local worktree. Detect and error on the old direct-push path.
**Why**: The GitHub Ruleset blocks direct pushes to main. The merge workflow must create PRs and wait for CI enforcement before merging.
**Scope**:
- IN: PR creation (gh pr create), CI polling (gh pr checks / gh run watch), merge (gh pr merge with auto flag), local worktree update to origin/main, old-path detection with clear error, edge case handling (422 stale HEAD, cli/cli 8206/8514)
- OUT: Hook removal (Story 1), documentation updates (Story 3), CI workflow creation (Epic A)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, running the merge workflow creates a PR, waits for CI to pass, merges via the GitHub API, and updates the local worktree to origin/main
  ← Satisfies: "revised merge-to-main.sh implements PR workflow"
- When this story is complete, existing worktree sessions attempting the old direct-push path receive a non-zero exit with actionable guidance directing them to the PR workflow
  ← Satisfies: "existing worktree sessions receive clear error message"
- When this story is complete, the merge workflow handles gh pr merge auto edge cases (stale HEAD 422, failed check errors) with explicit error detection and retry logic
  ← Satisfies: "revised merge-to-main.sh implements PR workflow"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Conflicting Assumptions] Current merge-to-main.sh phases include version_bump which creates a local commit on main. PR-based flow cannot create local commits on main — the merge happens remotely. Decide: pre-merge version bump on branch? post-merge CI Action? Skip version bump in PR flow?
- [Consumer Impact] end-session SKILL.md calls merge-to-main.sh with --bump flag. Sprint SKILL.md references it in Phase 8. Both assume synchronous local merge. PR-based flow is async (CI must pass before merge). Verify the new interface is compatible with callers, especially --bump, --resume, and the is_merged check in worktree cleanup.
- [Reliability] gh pr merge auto has documented failure modes (cli/cli 8206, 8514). Must implement explicit error detection and retry.
- [Scope Overlap] Both this story and Story 1 modify COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md. Coordinate changes to prevent merge conflicts — Story 1 removes gate steps, this story updates the "After Commit: Merging to Main" section.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
